properties.remark: Delayed report. Photos via Social Media confirm an Ice Jam on the Allagash River developed January 8-9th and continues. The jam begins near the public boat launch and extends upstream to 3 Mile Island. Photos show significant jumbled ice 8-14 inches thick. ALLM1 river gage shows a max stage of 12.96 feet. Gage continues to hover around 12.5ft. No flooding is occuring.
Update to ice jam which remains on the Allagash River, extending upstream for 7 miles. Jumbled ice chunks range 1.5 to 3.5 ft thick. No flooding was found at this time.
Photo of an ice jam on Dennys River looking south. No information on impacts, if any. Poster says ice is 4 to 6 feet below the bridge.
Quarter mile long ice jam. Just below the confluence of the St Croix stream. Water moving around jam.
Piscataquis County EMA reporting an Ice Jam at the Pleasant Street bridge over the Pleasant River. Pleasant River Road is closed due to backwater flooding. Water is rising behind the jam.
Flooding of some properties along the Medford Road due to water bypassing the ice jam. No structures threatened at this time.
Ice jam on the Mattawamkeag is causing flooding on parts of Bancroft Rd between Wytopitlock and South Bancroft. Road closed.
St. John River Ice Jam has grown overnight to over 4 miles long. The toe is located near Pelletier Brook and extends upstream of the Dickey Bridge. Minor low lying flooding has been reported.
Photos into Social Media shows an Ice Jam has developed in the Castle Hill bend of the Aroostook River. No flooding at this time.
Social media photos show flooding of Gardner Creek Road in the private section of the road in the town of Wade. Camps have been cut off but this is a common occurrence each Ice Out season.
Ice jam on the Aroostook flooding North Caribou Rd at the Caribou and Fort Fairfield town line.
St. John River, ice jam from the Allagash to St. Francis. Road right by the town line, specifically at 1788 Main Street, has water at road level. Probability of the road flooding is extremely high.
Ice jam extends 1.5 miles downstream. Gardner Creek Rd is flooded with the camps getting water as well.
The ice jam further upstream at the St Francis town line has released to this location and stopped again. No flooding at this time.
From location and about 1.5 miles up river is an ice jam. No flooding has been reported.
6 mile long ice jam at the St. John and St. Francis town line. Farmland around the St. John River is inundated but no roads threatened.
Delayed Report. Numerous trees down blocking the Pinkham Road near Mile Marker 54 near the Telos Road in the area of Fourth Lake.

Storm survey conducted on June 10th found wind damage along the Huber Road between mile mark 4 and 8 in T7 R8 Wels and T6 R8 Wels. 60 to 70 hardwood and softwood trees were damage. Several trees were snapped at the trunks and many uprooted. Estimated wind speeds of 60 to 70mph. Time estimated by radar data.
Trees down just south of Knowles Corner.
Single dead tree fell near where US route 2 intersects I95.
Trees down on Rt 212 near Clark Rd.
Tree down along East Blaine Road.
Tree down across wires at 341 Station Rd in Littleton.
Tree down across Ross Ridge Rd in Littleton.
Trees down across US Route 1 blocking traffic.
Tree down across Shaw Rd in Littleton.
Trees and wires down at 1432 Foxcroft Rd in Littleton.
Trees down near the intersection of Framingham and Carson roads.
Hail mostly nickel size, with a few quarter size.
Corrects previous hail report from 2 NNW Westfield. Hail mostly nickel size, with a few up to half dollar size.
Trees and powerlines down in the Madawaska, ME area. Time estimated by radar.
Multiple trees and powerlines down in the Portage, ME area. Time estimated by radar.
6-8 trees down across powerlines in the Grand Isle area. Time estimated by radar.
A tree was observed to be leaning on a powerline in the Caribou area. Time estimated by radar.
Telos Rd damaged and eroded with tree and other debris washed over/along the road indicative of swiftly flowing water. Time estimated 30 minutes after peak CREST unit streamflow estimates.
Trees and powerlines down in the Sangerville, ME area. Time estimated by radar.
Trees down on North Stage Coach Rd in Atkinson, ME. Time estimated by radar.
911 caller reported a tree down in East Millinocket. Time estimated by radar.
911 caller reported a tree down in the Howland area. Time estimated by radar.
Tree down on powerlines on Water St. Time estimated by radar.
4 healthy trees freshly down, one blocking half of the Telos Rd.
Corrects previous lightning report from 3 NE Danforth. Report on social media of lakeside homes and camps with trees down on or around them on East Grand Lake. Time estimated based on radar.
Tree down, no power lines involved.
Lightning strike. Tree fell on vehicle and down on Exeter Road with wires down.
Burning tree on wires at Pinkham Drive and Darling Road in Hudson.
Tree down in road on Old Town Road, near Rogers Market.
Tree on power lines on Exeter Road.
Low hanging wires and trees down on Greenfield Road.
Tree down across the road.
Quarter size hail in Ashland.
Large tree uprooted.
Corrects timing of previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 ESE Ashland. Trees down and pole snapped near Ashland High School closing road.
Trees down and pole snapped near Ashland High School closing road.
Tree down on Sly Brook Rd. Time estimated by radar.

Corrects previous flood report from 2 SE Baker Lake. Delayed Report. North Woods Assoc reported severe flooding damage to Baker Lake Road near mile 28. Gravel road was heavily damaged. Time based on peak MRMS flash crest. Radar estimates of 2-2.25 inches in 1 hour.
Delayed Report. Significant washout of Baker Lake Road near Sweeney Brook. This is a dirt/gravel road. Time based on peak MRMS flash crest. Radar estimates of 2-2.25 inches in 1 hour.

Delayed Report. Retired NHDOT employee reported significant road damage to the St. Aurelie Road near mile 25. This is a gravel/dirt road. Numerous culverts and ditches destroyed. Time based on peak MRMS flash crest. Radar estimates of 2-2.25 inches in 1 hour.
Tree down on powerlines.
Delayed report. Garland Fire reported tree down on Corinth Road.
A small cluster of big trees snapped at the edge of a farmers field. Time estimated by radar.
Report from social media. Tree down near Madawaska Lake shoreline. Large tree limb down as well. Time estimated based on radar.
Report on social media of trees uprooted and others snapped in half. Time estimated based on radar.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from Princeton. Washington County Emergency Management reports multiple trees down on Route 1 in Princeton. Time estimated based on radar.
Tree and powerlines down across a road in Princeton. Time estimated by radar.
A spotter reported trees down in Calais. Time estimated by radar.
Social media report of numerous pine trees down on Route 1 near Calais. Time estimated based on radar.
Washington County Emergency Management reports multiple trees down on Route 1 in Princeton. Time estimated based on radar.
Fire department reports 1 tree down which took down some wires. Time estimated by radar.
Mesonet station MDRM1 15 ESE Frenchboro.

Photos via social media confirms an Ice Jam has formed on the Pleasant River just upstream of the confluence with the Piscataquis River. No flooding reported at this time but water is backing up due to the jam.
